prompt: I want a markflow workflow that takes a `city` input, fetches the weather for it, then uses claude to write a one-sentence recommendation of whether it's good weather to walk a dog. Include the ability to pass the city at run time.

assertions.text: workflow.md validates cleanly via `markflow init` (exit 0, no parse/validation errors)
File contains an `# Inputs` section declaring a `city` parameter
`# Flow` section present with exactly one fenced mermaid block
`# Steps` section present; every node referenced in the flow has a matching `## <id>` heading and every `## <id>` heading appears in the flow
Includes at least one script step (```bash / ```sh / ```python / ```js) that performs the weather fetch
Includes at least one agent step (plain prose under a `##` heading, no fenced code block body) for the dog-walk recommendation
The agent step references the city via Liquid templating (e.g., `{{ city }}` or `{{ GLOBAL.* }}` populated from the fetch step) — not hard-coded
No use of banned patterns: `local`/`global` keys nested inside `RESULT`, unpaired `max:N`/`:max` edges, or unknown code-block language
